one_time_description: COVID-19 additional funding received from Indigenous Services Canada and other sources for public health measures, income assistance initiatives, and reopening costs
economic_notes: Nation operates 327938 Alberta Ltd, Kapawe'no First Nation Narrows Cultural Resort Inc., Kapawe'no First Nation Oil & Gas Company Ltd, and Kapawe'no First Nation Childcare Society. Nation is member of Lesser Slave Lake Indian Regional Council (LSLIRC) which negotiates funding agreements with Government of Canada.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Four fully consolidated subsidiaries: 327938 Alberta Ltd, Kapawe'no First Nation Narrows Cultural Resort Inc., Kapawe'no First Nation Oil & Gas Company Ltd, and Kapawe'no First Nation Childcare Society. Federal Funding - Department of Natural Resources of $1,499,900 was budgeted but $0 actual received in 2021 (was received in 2020). Trust funds of $5,102,243 held in Ottawa and other trust accounts are included in investments. COVID-19 funding of $2,280,900 received. Cash includes externally restricted ($132,432 Province of Alberta) and internally restricted ($2,235,205 special projects) amounts. No schedule of remuneration was included in the provided text. ISC-Health actual per segmented note was $1,994,080 but statement of operations shows $1,949,175 - using statement of operations figure. total_government_transfers calculated as LSLIRC $2,652,147 + ISC Health $1,949,175 + Province of Alberta $245,682 = $4,847,004. current_liabilities not separately disclosed in provided text.
